item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for critting!

  • Tbh, the revision feels worse personally. It doesn't even describe what the new photo depicts.
  • The idea is that -# refers to any photo, and that -(exact number) refers to a specific photo. That way, I never have to use a -1-(exact number) system that uses sub-sub-identifiers. SCP-5701-1-13 just doesn't look clean, but SCP-5701-13 is a little better.
  • The advanced description is there so that readers can skip it, but the in-universe Foundation can have specifics on how it functions. I know; it's unusual, but I thought that the Foundation would realistically look into the advanced details.
  • Admitted, testing was a hard one to write because I was trying to ramp it up over time vs. starting at full throttle. Even choosing subjects was a pain to do right since I didn't want just 10 D-Class in a row. As for the Albro reference, the idea is that it was inspired by the "embarrassing thing around your crush" gag. I'll revise that rn. Good catch!
  • Dialogue was one of my concerns while working on this. I tried, I did get crit on it, but I guess it still fell flat. Sorry.
  • The entire reason you would read the article is to get to the story, not to summarize the camera. The camera is just the catalyst, so deleting the story deletes the only good part of the article. You might as well tell me to start from scratch.

Sorry if I was a little defensive, but thank you for your crit anyway!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm afraid I didn't like this very much. The seed of the idea isn't bad, but there's a lot going wrong with the execution that leads me to downvote. I've included some examples below:

Examples
Examples

If a human subject is photographed by SCP-5701, the next time the subject performs an action they regret or consider to be a mistake, or shows regret about a previous action of the same nature, the SCP-5701-# instance in which they were photographed will be altered to depict the subject performing that action.

This is a fairly tortured sentence. I would suggest something along the lines of:

Any photograph made by SCP-5701 displays an anomalous propensity to alter its content, triggered by the subject of the photograph engaging in an action they will come to regret, or an action which is substantively similar to one they have come to regret previously.

The '#' nomenclature feels unnecessary. You can simply refer to them as -1 or whatever suits your fancy.

The advanced description feels unnecessary. If there is no thematic reason for only the first instance to morph, this is a needless complication. I likewise can't see a reason to include a description of what happens to multiple subjects, except to address the inevitable 'what if X' style comments in the discussion.

The experiment log contains bizarre examples: the D-class was predicted to regret recoiling from a joybuzzer? Reprimanding a janitor for forgetting their keys (hell, involving a janitor in an object test at all)? Who is Dr. Albro and why should I care? I could go on but the point, I hope, is made.

You damn sons-of-guns lied to me. You told me that this camera wouldn't even hurt me. Well, then it goes on to hurt, if not shred apart, my career. Next thing you know, the whole God-forsaken Foundation's getting spied on. Stop doing this to people. Now." - Dr. Marley, 3-31-2005, from an interview on the same day as his confrontation.

This dialogue feels very unnatural.

The final collapsible adds very little. Frankly, the entire inclusion of the PoI is so fundamentally aside from the description of the object that I think it harms the work. I understand that was the story you were trying to tell, but this is a situation where you want less direct explanation, not more. The reader can be trusted to find their way to the idea you had in mind, if you execute well enough. As it stands, I admit, you haven't, but hopefully some polish can improve this.

item.title: Re: I liked it.
item.content:encoded: Thx m8! I’m sorry, but I don’t remember anything like that. Even then, the willingness to read this is greatly appreciated!

item.title: I liked it.
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure I helped you and responded during the idea phase and helped guide you somewhere along the line, it was probably negative, but now this is for sure positive! Nice work +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the support!

I went with -# because that’s how most people designate separate instances, so I just kept it the same. The -1-# idea was still a good idea, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Omg I forgot I didn’t reply ;; I was kinda busy when I saw the message, so I told myself I’d reply later and never did, I’m so sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job! Needed a bit of a break from all of the meta and crazy skips lately, so this is appreciated! One thing, I recommend changing the designation of 5701-# to 5701-1 to designate all photos created, and then 5701-1-# would be a specific photo. At least that's what I've seen from other writings. Keep it up! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're gonna leave as soon as I make my next article?

Jk, but thanks for the support!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s been nice to see this rise out of the negatives. It’s not the most innovative concept for sure, but it’s a fun time, and a great first SCP.

I hope you take the criticism to heart and I am exited to see what you make next.

+1

item.title: Re: Really liked it until
item.content:encoded:
I heard that was one of my issues during crit, but it's hard to tell exactly where it is at any given time, and sometimes, it feels right but isn't. Could you help out and name a few examples?

Edit: Just figured I'd explain what I did and why if that's needed.
I don't know what MTFU Epsilon-6-13 did wrong. That felt as professional as possible.
Dr. Mason isn't interviewing; he's interrogating. He'll get harsh, but that's the point.
Dr. Dawkins is trying to get PoI-5701-3 to calm down. He needs to have a level of sympathy to keep him cool because a panic of any kind would just instantly shut the interview down.
If you still have any questions, ask and I might be able to answer!

Edit 2: So I made Dr. Mason a little less aggressive and more professional. Not much, but I figured I should just solve the problem for myself. Same with making Dr. Dawkins a little less attached. As for the remarks about his job… I figured they boosted the closing statement, so they'll stay in tact for now.

item.title: Really liked it until
item.content:encoded: This was a pretty good read. It wasn't anything groundbreaking, but it was still enjoyable enough. Unfortunately the point that really dipped for me was the interviews. They really didn't sound like foundation personnel talking and it felt really unprofessional. Other than that this was a solid read for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was actually inspired after I tried to write an article by starting with the fear of failure, then found out a camera would be neato. No Goosebumps here. But thanks for telling me about it!

You can have a shoutout spot in the support area. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this! I'm not sure why there's so many downvotes on this article, I personally think it was a good read. It kind of reminds me of a similar camera from the Goosebumps book "Say Cheese and Die," where the camera was actually the cause of the disasters. I'm not sure if that book inspired this or not, but regardless, it's a good article. Don't let those negative votes get you down! :)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I needed that. Thanks! Have a shoutout spot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed reading! Don't let the negative votes get you down! You're a good writer!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the revision! You've earned a spot in the shoutouts list!

Honestly, there were times where it felt like my "greatest weakness" would be resolved… and then it would happen again. I guess it's happening again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True. I PM'd the author about it, but no reply…
I just had to upload it today though because I could sense the Series VI tidal wave as soon as 5kon ended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm afraid that, to me, the approach to clinical tone here oftentimes ends up feeling exaggerated, overly verbose, and confusing to read.

Here's an example of how a confusing paragraph might be simplified:

+ Show
– hide block
Original

SCP-5701-# instances are any photographs created by SCP-5701. If any person, henceforth referred to as "subject," is photographed within an SCP-5701-# instance and later either do a present action that they regard as a "mistake" or have a noticeable regret of a past action, said SCP-5701-# instance will change to depict a scene of the subject making said mistake or regret. Additional specifics of the behavior of SCP-5701-# instances can be seen below:

Revised

SCP-5701-# are photographs created by SCP-5701. If a human subject is photographed by SCP-5701, the next time the subject performs an action they regret or consider to be a mistake, the SCP-5701-# in which they were photographed will be altered to depict that event. Additional details of this phenomenon can be seen below:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the main problem here is that another polaroid camera skip was posted very recently. On their own, both are good, however, yours being second really fast after the last one will make this difficult to succeed. Good luck man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SORRY!

But yeah, thanks for the catch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Psst you forgot to change the all of the SCP-XXXX's to SCP-5701 (and same for all the PoI's and stuff too)

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
+ Show Image Credits
- Hide Image Credits

This image is borrowed from:

Original Source: "Own work"
Creator: Jonathan Mauer
License: CC BY-SA 4.0

+ Show Shoutouts
- Hide Shoutouts
  • Critique
    • Velvetmorm (for first critique on idea)
    • Dr Prysin (for more refined critique on idea and inspiration for the use of Class C amnestics)
    • silicon_arrow (for IRC Chat critique)
    • MalyceGraves (for first greenlight)
    • Calibri Bold (for second greenlight)
    • Jak (for reminding me to focus the reader on what matters)
    • Habitual_Snubnose (for a HUGE formatting change and an unofficial A-OK for uploading)
    • AgentAX (for additional critique and an unofficial A-OK for uploading)
    • Doctor Fullham (for dialogue focused critique and a reminder to avoid plot convenience)
    • Sevatar (for brutally honest formality critique)
    • Cyvstvi (for brutally honest formality critique)
    • PeppersGhost (for revising a confusing paragraph for me. See below!)
    • Carrington (for pointing out some rough usage of Dr. Albro. See on page 2!)
  • Positive Support
    • Vincent Van Gone (for emotional consoling during idea phase. Yes, I needed it, and yes, it helped.)
    • Zyn (for putting up with all my annoying incidents.)
    • Cerastes (for convincing me to not delete my account. That happened. Don't ask.)
    • Nurse_Saccharine (See below!)
    • TheMonkeyBusiness (for showing similar stories as well. See below!)
    • 1KaL (for a reminder to take the critique to heart. Send some in! See below!)

This one's my first officially uploaded idea onto the mainsite. Yes, it's been greenlighted; see the shoutouts. Same for critique (although I'll gladly take more!)

Edit: I confess. I self-tagged the page. I decided to do this after seeing that my article did not register in the system as an SCP, so I tried to fix that, as well as add in other tags to the best of my ability. Sorry if I messed it up.
